critique_responses.title: MVP scope unrealistic for 8 engineers in 3 months
critique_responses.rationale: Critique is valid. Four substantial systems in parallel is overreach. Will reduce Q1 scope to Agent Harness MVP + Basic Eval Foundation. Gateway and full Observability deferred to Q2.
critique_responses.changes_made: Revised Q1 roadmap: (1) Agent Harness MVP - 4 engineers, 10 weeks; (2) Eval Foundation - 3 engineers, 10 weeks; (3) Basic Tracing - 1 engineer, 6 weeks (lightweight, not full observability)

critique_responses.title: Self-verification latency impact underestimated
critique_responses.rationale: Valid concern. LLM-based verification does add significant latency. Will implement tiered approach as suggested.
critique_responses.changes_made: Added tiered verification architecture: Tier 1 (rules-based, <50ms) for all outputs; Tier 2 (LLM-based, 300-500ms) only for low-confidence or high-stakes outputs. Defined latency budget: verification must not exceed 25% of base latency.

critique_responses.title: Self-verification doubles LLM costs in worst case
critique_responses.rationale: Cost impact is real. Confidence-based routing is the right mitigation.
critique_responses.changes_made: Updated cost model: Tier 2 verification expected for <20% of outputs. Cost reduction target revised to 30% (from 40%) to account for verification overhead. Added metric: verification cost ratio tracked per agent.

critique_responses.title: Reliability positioning may not be defensible
critique_responses.rationale: Agree that reliability alone is not a moat. However, disagree that we must pick a vertical now. The moat is in the evaluation datasets and self-correction algorithms, not the positioning claim.
critique_responses.changes_made: Added strategic bet: 'Proprietary Eval Datasets' - build domain-specific golden datasets and correction patterns that competitors cannot easily replicate. Pnyx community signals reinforce machine-checkable gates as a differentiation vector (80% of quality issues caught by machine checks).

critique_responses.title: Team composition unclear
critique_responses.rationale: Valid gap. Cannot execute without skill clarity.
critique_responses.changes_made: Added team composition section: Required: 2 infra/platform, 2 ML/LLM specialists, 2 backend, 1 frontend, 1 DevOps. Current gaps: ML/LLM (need 1 hire Q1), infra (need 1 hire Q1). Roadmap now shows role assignments per milestone.

critique_responses.title: Sandbox security model not specified
critique_responses.rationale: Critical gap. Security model must be defined before MVP.
critique_responses.changes_made: Added sandbox specification: gVisor for lightweight isolation, Firecracker for multi-tenant production. Resource limits: 2GB RAM, 1 vCPU, 60s max execution, no network by default. Security review scheduled for Week 8 of Q1. Threat model document added to Q1 deliverables.

critique_responses.title: Semantic caching hit rate assumption may not transfer
critique_responses.rationale: Prudent to validate before committing infrastructure.
critique_responses.changes_made: Reduced target to 40% hit rate until validated. Added pilot study to Q1 (Week 6-8): sample production traffic to benchmark actual hit rates. Gateway caching moved to Q2, dependent on pilot results.

critique_responses.title: Eval coverage metric lacks definition
critique_responses.changes_made: Defined behavior taxonomy: (1) Tool Selection, (2) Output Generation, (3) Error Handling, (4) Termination Logic, (5) State Management. Coverage matrix: each behavior category requires unit tests + integration tests + golden dataset examples. Metric: % of taxonomy cells with passing tests.

critique_responses.title: Multi-provider dependency creates operational complexity
critique_responses.rationale: Multi-provider is strategic. Single-provider lock-in is a business risk. The operational complexity is acceptable given the risk mitigation. Pnyx community signals show structured contracts (JSON Schema) reduce integration failures.
critique_responses.changes_made: No change to multi-provider strategy. Added: standardized provider interface contract (JSON Schema) to minimize per-provider integration burden.

critique_responses.title: Agent Marketplace in Q4 is premature
critique_responses.rationale: Agree. Marketplace before platform validation is putting the cart before the horse.
critique_responses.changes_made: Marketplace deferred to Year 2. Q4 focus changed to: Platform Hardening, SOC2 preparation, and Enterprise Pilot program.

q1.description: Core runtime with termination conditions, tiered self-verification (rules-based), artifact persistence
Behavior taxonomy, golden dataset infrastructure, basic quality metrics, CI integration
Threat model, gVisor integration, security review
Benchmark semantic caching on sample traffic

q2.description: Reasoning chain viz, cost attribution, alert framework
LLM-based verification for high-stakes outputs, confidence routing

q3.description: Coordination patterns, shared state, handoff protocols
LLM-evaluated quality scoring (pnyx pattern), cross-artifact consistency

q4.description: Performance tuning, cost optimization, operational maturity
Security audit, penetration testing, SOC2 prep

decision_log_additions.decision: Reduce Q1 scope to Harness + Eval only; defer Gateway to Q2
decision_log_additions.rationale: 8 engineers cannot deliver 4 systems in parallel. Focus on core value (reliability runtime + eval) first.
alternatives_considered: Deliver Gateway MVP instead of full Eval

decision_log_additions.decision: Implement tiered verification (rules-based default, LLM-based for high-stakes)
decision_log_additions.rationale: Balances reliability with latency/cost constraints. Rules-based catches 80% of issues per pnyx community data.

decision_log_additions.decision: Maintain multi-provider strategy despite operational complexity
decision_log_additions.rationale: Single-provider lock-in is unacceptable business risk. Structured contracts (JSON Schema) mitigate integration burden.
Platform must be validated before building ecosystem features.

decision_log_additions.decision: Use gVisor for sandbox with Firecracker for multi-tenant production
decision_log_additions.rationale: gVisor is lighter for development; Firecracker provides stronger isolation for production multi-tenancy.
